query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "dragmesh-2: physically plausible dexterous hand-object interaction wit" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (dexterous hand-object interaction) and domain (controlling high-dimensional robotic hands) as the user's question, even though it focuses on a reinforcement learning approach rather than the specific "DragMesh-2" physics-based method. It provides the necessary canonical prior work on the challenges and solutions for dexterous manipulation that a literature review on extending such methods would require.
